title: Reverse String
description: Write a function that reverses a string. The input string is given as an array of characters `s`. You must do this by modifying the input array in-place with O(1) extra memory.

starter.python:
def reverse_string(s):
    """
    Do not return anything, modify s in-place instead.
    """
    pass

hints: The entire logic for reversing a string is based on using the opposite directional two-pointer approach.
Swap the first and last elements, then move pointers inward.
solution: Use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end. Swap the characters at these pointers and move them towards each other until they meet.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string `s` containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if: Open brackets are closed by the same type of brackets, and open brackets are closed in the correct order.
hints: When you encounter an opening bracket, push it to the stack.
When you encounter a closing bracket, check if the top of the stack matches.
solution: Push opening brackets onto a stack. When a closing bracket appears, pop the stack and ensure it matches the corresponding opening type. If the stack is empty at the end, the string is valid.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array `nums`, return `true` if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return `false` if every element is distinct.
hints: A Hash Set is a great way to check for duplicates.
Alternatively, you could sort the array first.
solution: Iterate through the array and add each number to a Set. If a number is already in the Set, return true. If the loop finishes, return false.

title: Maximum Subarray
description: Given an integer array `nums`, find the subarray with the largest sum, and return its sum.
hints: Try to solve this using Kadane's Algorithm.
If the current sum becomes negative, it's better to start fresh from the next element.
solution: Maintain a 'current_sum' and 'max_sum'. Iterate through the array, adding the current element to 'current_sum'. If 'current_sum' becomes less than the current element itself, reset 'current_sum' to that element. Update 'max_sum' at each step.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists `list1` and `list2`. Merge the two lists into one sorted list. The list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists.
hints: Use a dummy node to act as the head of the merged list.
Compare the current nodes of both lists and attach the smaller one to the result.
solution: Create a dummy node and a pointer. Iterate while both lists have nodes, attaching the node with the smaller value to the pointer's next and advancing that list. After the loop, attach any remaining nodes from either list.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `t` is an anagram of `s`, and `false` otherwise.
hints: An anagram must have the same length.
Count the frequency of each character in both strings.
solution: First, check if lengths are equal. Use a frequency map (or an array of size 26 for lowercase English) to count characters in `s` and decrement for `t`. If all counts are zero, it's an anagram.
space_complexity: O(1) (since the alphabet size is constant)
